name: Visby
summary: Visby is a tiny medieval town on the Swedish island of Gotland, wrapped in a thick stone wall over 700 years old. Its crooked lanes are full of ruined churches and climbing roses.
funFacts: Visby's medieval Ringmuren wall stretches about 3.4 kilometres around the old town and still has 27 of its tall watchtowers from the 1200s standing.
The real Villa Villekulla house from the Pippi Longstocking films sits inside Kneippbyn park, where kids can climb on the roof just like Pippi.
Every August Visby hosts Medieval Week, when thousands of visitors arrive in armour and gowns for jousting, sword fights, jesters and torch-lit markets.
Around the coast of Gotland stand strange limestone towers called raukar, carved by waves over millions of years — the tallest is 27 metres high.
The seas around Gotland are home to the chunky Baltic grey seal, one of the biggest seals in the world, with bull seals weighing as much as a pony.
thingsToDo: Walk the top of the medieval Ringmuren wall and count its 27 ancient watchtowers.
Step inside St Mary's Cathedral, finished in 1225, the only old Visby church still in use.
Explore the giant roofless ruin of St Karin's church, where ivy and roses climb the walls.
Picnic in Almedalen park beside the harbour and watch sailboats come and go.
Find rose tunnels and tropical trees in the cliffside Botaniska Trädgården garden.
Take a trip to Fårö island to see the spooky raukar sea-stacks rising from the beach.

wildlife.name: Gotland Pony (Gotlandsruss)
wildlife.description: Small, tough native pony of Gotland that has roamed the island's forests for thousands of years.

wildlife.name: Baltic Grey Seal
wildlife.description: Big spotty seal with a long horse-like nose that loves to sunbathe on Gotland's rocks.

wildlife.name: Common Eider
wildlife.description: Chunky sea duck with a sloping green-and-white head that bobs around the Baltic coast.

wildlife.name: Mute Swan
wildlife.description: Huge white bird with an orange bill that glides across Visby's harbour and ponds.

images.alt: A wide view of Visby's old town from the clifftop, showing red-tiled roofs huddled inside the medieval ring wall by the blue Baltic Sea.
Visby's St Mary's Cathedral with its dark pointy spires rising above the red rooftops of the old town.
The tall roofless arches of St Karin's medieval church ruin in the middle of Visby.
Visby's chunky stone Gunpowder Tower (Kruttornet), the oldest part of the medieval city wall, standing by the harbour.
Fiskargränd, a tiny cobbled Visby alley with old wooden houses dripping in pink climbing roses.
Tall limestone sea stacks (raukar) standing like stone giants on the Langhammars beach on Fårö, Gotland.
A small chestnut Gotland pony (Gotlandsruss) standing in a paddock.
A big spotted Baltic grey seal stretching on a sunny rocky shore.
A male common eider duck with a sloping black-and-white head floating on calm water.
An adult mute swan with a bright orange bill gliding on water.
